answer:
The coefficient of performance (COP) for a reversible refrigerator is given by \( \text{COP} = \frac{T_c}{T_h - T_c} \), where temperatures are in absolute units (Rankine). Converting: \( 36^\circ\text{F} = 496^\circ\text{R} \), \( 0^\circ\text{F} = 460^\circ\text{R} \), and \( 65^\circ\text{F} = 525^\circ\text{R} \).  

The work required is inversely proportional to the COP, so the cost factor is the ratio of the COPs:  
\[
\frac{\text{COP}_{\text{fridge}}}{\text{COP}_{\text{freezer}}} = \frac{496/(525-496)}{460/(525-460)} = \frac{496/29}{460/65} \approx \frac{17.10}{7.077} \approx 2.42.
\]  
This rounds to
